title: OT Security Assessment Framework
when_to_use: Use OT Security Assessment Framework to develop professional-grade ot security assessment framework capabilities at Mastery level. This recipe is part of the Specialist Domains pillar and integrates with linked missions OM-M08.

steps.title: OT Environment Study
steps.instruction: Research target OT environment (e.g., power generation SCADA). Study architecture: HMI systems, PLCs, network topology, safety interlocks, operational technology. Identify: device vendors (Siemens, ABB), protocols (Modbus TCP, Profinet), network segmentation, backup/redundancy systems. Create architecture diagram with data flows.
steps.tip: OT systems have decades-old designs optimized for safety + reliability, not security. Understand why before assessing.

steps.title: Safety-First Assessment Methodology
steps.instruction: Design assessment that does NOT impact operations: no network scanning that could disrupt equipment, no experimental injections into control systems, no physical manipulation. Document: what you WILL test (passive reconnaissance, non-invasive security checks), what you WILL NOT do (load testing, exploit testing, physical intrusion). Get explicit approval for every test. Include kill switch procedures.
steps.tip: OT safety incidents can kill people. Conservative assessment is professional responsibility.

steps.title: IT/OT Boundary Testing
steps.instruction: Test segmentation between IT (corporate network) and OT (operational systems): verify firewall rules, analyze allowed traffic, test for unauthorized IT->OT connections, check for OT->IT data exfiltration. Document: boundary effectiveness, potential bridges (engineer laptops, mobile devices), legitimate but risky connections (remote vendor access).
steps.tip: Boundary testing is non-disruptive: use network monitoring + firewall logs.

steps.title: OT-Specific Threat Scenarios
steps.instruction: Develop 3 threat scenarios specific to African power utility OT: (1) remote network compromise of engineer workstations + lateral movement to SCADA, (2) insider threat with access to control network, (3) supply chain attack via firmware update server. Map each scenario to ATT&CK for ICS framework. Quantify: risk, impact, detection difficulty.
steps.tip: Tailor scenarios to client environment and threat landscape. African context: state-sponsored interest in infrastructure.

steps.title: OT-Specific Assessment Report
steps.instruction: Create assessment report template: findings organized by domain (architecture, IT/OT boundary, device security, network monitoring, incident response), severity with operational impact scoring (not just "critical"), remediation with implementation guidance, OT-aware recommendations (don't demand immediate patches if they require downtime). Include photos, network diagrams, evidence.
steps.tip: OT reports require different messaging than IT reports. Focus on operational resilience, not just vulnerability count.

steps.title: Client Education Package
steps.instruction: Create executive education for utility management: presentation (non-technical overview of OT security, threats to power infrastructure, cost of downtime), FAQ (answers to management questions: "Why can't we just patch?", "What if we upgrade to cloud SCADA?"), guidance (prioritized action plan, vendor relationships, certification programs). Make it compelling without being alarmist.
steps.tip: Utility executives care about business continuity. Frame security as operational resilience.
